siphon 0.8.0

    • Extending siphon with Custom Backends
  • Changelog

Consume one ready result from a pipeline

Source: R/inspect.R
pump_pop.Rd

pump_pop() removes and returns the next ready item from the terminal stage's output buffer, or NULL when nothing is ready. This consumes the item: it will not appear in the results of a later pump_run(), and responsibility for its lifecycle transfers to you. For managed sources (see pump_source()), call x$item_commit(id, data) and x$item_release(id) after successfully handling the item, exactly as pump_run() does; use pump_peek() instead when you only want to look.

Usage

pump_pop(x)

Arguments

x

A pump object.

Value

A list with id, idx, data, and ok, or NULL when no item is ready.

See also

Other inspection: pump_peek(), pump_step()

Examples

p <- 1:5 |> pump(function(x) x * 2, backend = "main")
pump_step(p)
#> <pump (1)>
#> ┌─ source   1/5
#> ├─ stage 1 main
#> │    wrk [-----] 0/1   buf [#----] 1/5   done 1   err 0
#> │    fn 0.0ms/it   crd 1.0ms/bt   wrk 100% stv 0% blk 0%
#> └─ sink   0/5
v <- pump_pop(p)
v$data
#> [1] 2
